description: Added counts for k=13, 14, and 15 (where feasible): E(13)=195, E(14)=51, E(15)=13 at N=10¹⁰. The fitted quadratic-log model yields: log E(k) = −0.0304(±0.0017)k² + 1.732(±0.021)k − 2.32(±0.12), R² = 0.993. Hensley's upper and lower bounds for {1,2,k} are now cited for k = 3–15 and show the empirical exception counts are below Hensley's upper bound for all tested k.
